prose: Fukui reactivity indices for phenol were computed using the fukui-reactivity skill in chemkit. Method: DFT, tier 'standard' (B3LYP/def2-TZVP), PySCF RKS backend, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Exact four-centre integrals (no density fitting). Geometry: user-supplied phenol.xyz, not re-optimized at this level. Partial charges: Mulliken (PySCF). The N-1 cation state was charge +1 / multiplicity 2 (doublet); the N+1 anion state was charge -1 / multiplicity 2 (doublet). All three SCF runs converged. Integrity check: trustworthy=true; f+ sum=1.0000, f- sum=1.0000 (charge conservation satisfied). No warnings reported. The headline value reported (2.1028319) is the largest f+ index, located on C3 (atom 3, 1-based), identifying it as the most electrophilic site (preferred for nucleophilic attack). The most nucleophilic site is O1 (atom 1) with f- = 0.16201477, consistent with electrophilic aromatic substitution directing effects of the hydroxyl group activating ortho/para ring carbons.
